metadata.mode: prospective; outcome unknown at lock time; decision citable under ANY outcome
metadata.question: Does a new justitia delay/t_irrev factorial sweep support a speed-limit boundary in R = delay / t_irrev? (partial isolation over existing dials; the full five-dial isolation named in the essay remains future work)
metadata.metric: Monotonicity in R and equal-R permanence spread on a post-lock grid over existing delay/t_irrev dials.

dial_reconnaissance.author_decision_needed: Approve this as an existing-dials J-N2, or specify a separate substrate-change phase before lock.

outcome_branches.PASS: No monotonicity violation above tolerance and equal-R permanence spread <= tolerance; cite only existing-dials speed-ratio support.
outcome_branches.FAIL: Any monotonicity violation above tolerance or equal-R spread > tolerance; speed-limit account fails under this existing-dials protocol.
outcome_branches.INCONCLUSIVE: Seed policy inadmissible or run failure; publish as-is.

metadata.what_was_not_shown: Independent variation of propagation speed, observation latency, intervention latency, and recovery rate is not shown unless new substrate dials are specified before lock.
